## **![](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/media/sm4pyvak/beethoven-piano-sonatas-nos-28-32.png)**

## **Beethoven** Piano Sonatas Nos 28-32

**Paul Wee** _pf_(BIS)

Having hugely impressed in virtuosic but lesser-recorded repertoire, pianist Paul Wee now demonstrates brilliance of colour and technique in Beethoven’s final sonatas, the most core of repertoire.

[**Read the _Gramophone_ review**](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/review/beethoven-the-last-piano-sonatas-paul-wee)

**Jonathan Biss** _pf_ **Swedish RSO / Malin Broman; Omer Meir Wellber**(Orchid)

Jonathan Biss’s ongoing survey of Beethoven’s piano concertos – this time No 4 – paired with a modern companion offers another superb instalment.

## **Purcell** Dido and Aeneas

_Sols incl_ **DiDonato & Spyres; Il Pomo d’Oro / Maxim Emelyanychev**

A performance of real poignancy and beauty; Joyce DiDonato and Michael Spyres are superb, with Il Pomo d’Oro and Maxim Emelyanychev as highly sensitive partners.

## **JS Bach**‘King of Kings’ – Orchestral Transcriptions by Andrew Davis

**BBC Philharmonic Orchestra / Martyn Brabbins, Andrew Davis**(Chandos)

On this splendid tribute to Sir Andrew Davis, his Bach transcriptions – initially by him and then completed by Martyn Brabbins – are beautifully realised by the BBC Philharmonic.

[**Read the _Gramophone_ review**](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/review/js-bach-king-of-kings-orch-davis)

**Ryo Terakado** _vn_ **Fabio Bonizzoni** _hpd_(Challenge Classics)

Violinist Ryo Terakado and harpsichord Fabio Bonizzoni make a wonderfully engaging pair, their glorious instrumental sound and evident rapport shining in CPE Bach’s music.
